reviews.review_text: en suite lacked shelving and had no toilet paper or hand soap
Great having own ensuite to keep stuff in

I had a very good stay at Marrara Gardens Caravan Park. I was warmly welcomed and the check-in and check-out process was very easy, with everything clearly explained.

I used the pool a lot, which was my favourite part of the caravan park. The location is also excellent, close to both the city and the airport.

The ensuite was simple but had everything I needed, and I found it very comfortable for my stay. Another thing I really enjoyed was the atmosphere of the place, with lots of trees, many birds around, and very pleasant weather.

I would definitely recommend it to anyone looking for a practical, well-located, peaceful place with great service!

Marrara Caravan Park is absolutely awesome.
It is very clean and staff are amazing and friendly. This is conveniently located and a perfect space for us to park our caravan.
It was awesome the time we spent there, relaxing and peaceful.
All amenities that you would need on a stay, clean and in a very good location, thumbs up for the staff who were super friendly and helpful!
All amenities that you would need on a stay, clean and in a very good location, thumbs up for the staff who were super friendly and helpful!

We had a great stay at Marrara Gardens Caravan Park. The park is clean, quiet and in a very convenient location close to the airport and the city. The staff were friendly and welcoming, and the facilities were well maintained. Highly recommend for anyone visiting Darwin!

We had a good stay at this caravan park in Marrara. Everything was very clean. The large pool was great, and the staff were friendly and helpful. The location was also convenient and close to everything we needed. Overall, we really enjoyed our stay and would stay here again.

We had a fantastic stay at Marrara Caravan Park. The park is very well maintained, clean, and peaceful, making it a great place to relax. The staff were incredibly welcoming and helpful throughout our stay. The location is also very convenient and perfect for parking our caravan. We truly enjoyed our time there and would happily stay again!

Park is old and not really aesthetically pleasing, but it works. They never did do many updates people  stated years ago apart from the pool and big wall at the front, rest of the park is all original. Prices are at a premium today compared to last year. Staying at Lee Point Caravan Park instead which we've stayed before and is quote good.

Apparently park is closed on Sunday
No way to check in - no hours stated
Poor service

Dont trust these owners. We made a booking for the next year paid our deposit and thought all was good. last week we received email to say the rates have all gone up. Our  bill increased by over $500 for a three week stay. The owners believe they can increase rates whenever they feel like it even when there is a contract in place. we will pick a more reputable business for our holiday.

Warning to all this Park will accept your booking and deposit and then put the prices up. the owners do not believe that when you book and pay a deposit that it is a binding contract. I am in current process with consumer affairs Darwin but dont think i will stay with them anyway. This has never happened to us before and i dont trust them for a minute.
Our first CP with our own ensuite, not just us but every site has one. Nice pool and surrounds and great staff with local knowledge. Safe and secure

Worst customer service !!
Never been spoken too so rudely in my life, this caravan park shuts at 4pm on a Tuesday. So if you are a tourist just arriving don’t bother with this place.
Management speaks rudely & then switches phones to answering machine.

Terrible service, arrived at 4.10pm  the gates were closed so we rang the office, the man who answered the phone abruptly said we are closed and you have to book, then hung up on me. I rang back and he had flicked the phone to the answering service.

I would not recommend this pace to anyone.  Arrogant jerk
A great caravan park close to Darwin city. Each site has its own ensuite. Older park but everything spotlessly clean and a beautiful big sparkling pool to cool off in.
Nice side, each side has his own ensuite. The pool is amazing. Unfortunately there is no shared area/camp kitchen. Fair price.

Excellent resort style pool area and very well located for visiting Darwin attractions.
Ensuite sites were a little small and awkward but given the age it’s acceptable with today’s big vans. The private ensuites however were dated but functioned just fine.
Quiet park overall, lots of grey nomads and a few long termers. Cheers to one friendly long termer for doing a great job keeping the grass green in the big open area, the kids enjoyed some lush grass for a change during the dry.
Road noise picks up during the morning commute as well as the odd aeroplane or noisy residents from the nearby suburb. All acceptable noise levels during the night though

Fantastic Caravan Park. Very Large caravan bays, all with ensuite,  very well kept, green grass, several shaded sites, fantastic pool. Very good security with gates, sensor lights and cameras.
The guys that run this park are very helpful, friendly, and have good local knowledge on places to go in Darwin.
Distance to Darwin centre is only about 15 minutes.
This is by far the best park we’ve stayed at in all our travels.  Cheaper than others, bigger sites, and reasonably close to town.
We enjoyed this park so much we extended our stay from 4 nights to two weeks.  No hesitation recommending this park.
Well be back next time in Darwin. No need to waste time  look around. Great pool. Having your own ensuite fantastic( don't have to hear others doing number 2s) good size sites
Well located ,reasonably priced compared to others, considering all sites have their own ensuits

The roads around the park need redoing,
Other than that it's old but all bearable
Great place to stay. Coles to the left Woollies to the right. Bus stop out the front. All sites are ensuites. Mark & Scott run the park so well. Make sure if you want extra time, do it straight away.

Great place we booked in for a week but ended staying a month
Mark and Scott are doing a awesome job running this business
Close to Darwin and everything thing else

Average. Very old. Needs updating esp for the price. Even the roads inside complex are rough n patched up.
Close to airport and very noisy.

Good location, no unpowered sites, so had to go powered, a bit  crammed in but have own ensuite which was a new experience and handy not having to do a hike to amenities.
Had a nice size pool area too.

We had a powered site. All sites have on suites. The bathrooms are lovely and clean. The park is beautifully maintained, green and spacious. It’s 15 mins to the museums and foreshore. Service is excellent with warm, friendly management. The pool is lovely too! Definitely recommend.

Marrara Caravan Park Darwin. Initially stayed on/ off for the past 3/4 years. Ideal for myself being a fly in/out to various communities,  a lot cheaper and secure than parking at the airport. Each site has their own ablution. Always quiet and the amenities such as pool a sight for sore eyes. Laundry always clean never had to que. Secure gate adds to safety, security. Credit to returning on site manager Mark and Scott

Conveniently located, this caravan park has all the standard amenities you'd expect. While some guests noted the proximity to the airport, we found the plane noise to be minimal and not an issue at all. The only downside was the solar hot water system—it didn’t quite have the capacity to reliably service the ensuite facilities for two sites, which was a bit inconvenient.
Beautiful camp ground with clean ensuites, great laundry and lovely staff. Highly recommend.

All ensuite sites. Nice hot water. Grass well maintained.
Easy access. Friendly staff.

We came for a week & stayed a Month, great people, great location , bus stop at your doorstep, not to mention a beautiful pool, highly recommend this Park & your own facilities too,
We hope to see you again Mark & Scott 🥰

Great location close to shops and city - even better if you want the footy. Sites are clean and tidy. Powered sites only and every site has a private ensuite (although you need to provide your own loo roll). Reverse in sites with plenty of shade. The pool is one of the best I've seen in a caravan park with wheelchair (ramp) and stair access a ledge seat along the edges and a shallow young kids part. Plenty of deck chairs and shade or sun as desired.
Fantastic Caravan Park with absolutely lovely management Mark and Scott can't do enough for there guests and keep all amenities clean and sparkling 😀 👍 👌 ✨️
Sites only quite peaceful
Good

Great park! Nice clean facilities, every site has an ensuite which was a game changer. Very safe for the kids with a great pool and a kiddie pool too. We used Marrara as a base and stayed here 3 seperate times within 3 weeks. The owners were more than happy to accommodate a late check out and were friendly and helpful whenever we saw them. Thanks Marrara!
Good caravan park with wonderful pool . The facilities are old but clean

Fantastic park to stay with awesome managers. Ensuite, clothesline standard at your site. Beautiful big swimming pool to enjoy and relax in. Located close to shopping centre etc. We will stay again when next in Darwin. Thanks guys 🙂

Great caravan park! Very dog friendly, loads of big open grassy areas to walk and play with our dog. They even have a pet specific washing machine which was perfect for us. Amazing pool area to relax at and nice lawn and alfresco area. Facilities are top notch as well! 4 working washing machines, 2 dryers and the pet washing machine too. They even swap gas bottles and have many dumpsters for rubbish. Each site has an en suite bathroom and little drive/parking area, with concrete slab next to caravan parking spot and then lots of grass around the sites. Security is also well thought out, with a high walled perimeter fence and driveways gates that are locked at night. Staff were super friendly and helpful, we extended our stay in Darwin and ended up staying 10 days.

This is an unusual set up with your own ensuite.  I think it was an excellent concept.  Mark & Scott ate fabulous - very helpful and professional and made us very welcome.  They are super busy and we were lucky to get in.  We have used the bus service to get into Darwin CBD - across road to head in - coming back bus-stop in on same side of complex.   Pool is wonderful.  I couldn’t hear traffic noise nor planes at all. We had dinner at Parap Tavern $19 steak night on Wednesday - delicious - also booked in for massage & facial at Chantra at Parap - Uber home.  Tomorrow we are using car to explore further afield.  Thanks boys for a great couple of days.
Nice park and great pool

Great place to stay in
And safe

Avoid this place at all circumstances - checked in 22/11 and given access code for gate after 6pm, tried to enter at 9.30pm and access code didn’t work, no emergency number available at gate, had to spend the night sleeping in car elsewhere! Next day tried to contact but office not open for another 2 days! Phoned to report problem and given another access code which who knows if will work tonight! Asked about refund for the night I spent on street, advised to speak to office which doesn’t open for another 2 days!!! Avoid avoid avoid!
Neat and clean

Very comfortable caravan park. Well looked after by Mark the caretaker and his partner Michelle. Nothing is to much trouble even helped out with minor repairs to my caravan. Great amenities including en suites and a beautiful pool. Recommended.

Our 6 weeks in Marrara Caravan Park was very enjoyable. Michelle & Mark were very helpful. Ensuite was clean. Central to everything we needed. If you want 5 star go elsewhere. Grass, pet friendly and relaxed atmosphere!

They take payment for powered site as soon as booking is made.  We stayed twice for a night on seperate occasions two weeks apart.  The first stay Arrived at 550pm and office was already shut even though they said they would be there until 6.  Receptionist blunt and rude.  They don’t supply any toilet paper for ensuite. They also take $100 as a “security payment” and we didn’t get it back for a week,  Despite only having a powered caravan site for one night.
Can understand hotels doing this but a powered site?  What can be damaged when using our own van and the accomodation is already paid for?
Positives are the location, close to airport and short drive to Darwin, and the ensuite even if there is no loo paper.
Worst experience of my life not only is the place dodgy but management is too. Be careful coming here
